scorecard.summary: Strong CRUD for Users with bulk ops, audit logs with export. Feature-gated dashboards are read-only without management actions. No user export, no failed job management.

Excellent structural uniformity (>90% same layout pattern). Shared components well-used for stats/tables/pagination. Chart components exist but underutilized — most pages build charts inline. Minor spacing and badge inconsistencies.
Strong loading states, confirmation dialogs, debounced search, URL-persisted filters, accessible pagination, and empty states. ErrorBoundary exists but unused. Keyboard shortcuts only on Users Index.

Excellent self-action prevention, impersonation safety, SQL injection protection, CSRF, XSS prevention. AuditLog user relationship missing withTrashed. toggleAdmin route missing withTrashed. Single-admin lockout risk.
Comprehensive audit logging with IP/user-agent. No before/after value capture for mutations. No failed job retry from admin. Rate limiting thorough.
Good differentiation across pages — not cookie-cutter. Feature-gated sections are read-only dashboards without management. No data integrity tools.

priority_actions: ADM-OPS-001: Add before/after value capture to AuditService for admin mutations
ADM-PM-001: Add user CSV export to admin Users Index
ADM-QA-001: Fix toggleAdmin route missing withTrashed or hide UI for deleted users
ADM-UX-001: Wrap AdminLayout children in ErrorBoundary
ADM-QA-009: Add minimum-admin-count check to prevent single-admin lockout
ADM-QA-002: Add withTrashed to AuditLog::user() relationship
ADM-DES-001: Migrate chart pages to shared AdminCharts components

findings.title: No user CSV export from admin panel
findings.description: The admin Users Index has search, filter, sort, and bulk deactivate but no export capability. Audit logs have CSV export. The CsvExport support class exists but is not used for users. Admins commonly need to export user lists for reporting, compliance, or migration.

evidence.note: No export method exists
Export pattern exists for audit logs but not replicated for users

implementation.approach: Add exportUsers method to AdminUsersController following the audit log export pattern
verification: php artisan test --filter=AdminUser
Verify CSV download works with filters

findings.title: Feature-gated admin sections are read-only dashboards with no management actions
findings.description: The Webhooks, API Tokens, Social Auth, Two-Factor, and Notifications admin pages show aggregate statistics but provide no way to manage individual records. An admin cannot view/revoke a specific token, disable a webhook endpoint, or unlink a social account. These are analytics-only views.

evidence.note: Single __invoke method returning stats only, no CRUD
evidence.proof: Returns stats and most_active list but no revoke/view individual token capability

evidence.note: Stats dashboard only — no endpoint management, no delivery retry
evidence.proof: Returns stats, delivery_chart, recent_failures but no manage/retry actions

implementation.approach: Add list/detail views with management actions for the most critical resources (tokens, webhook endpoints)
changes: Add token list with revoke action
Add webhook endpoint list with disable/enable
verification: Feature tests for new admin actions

findings.title: No failed job management from admin panel
findings.description: The System page shows failed_jobs count but provides no way to view, retry, or delete failed jobs. Admins must use CLI (php artisan queue:retry) for job management. This is a common admin panel gap.

evidence.note: Shows pending_jobs and failed_jobs count but no actions

implementation.approach: Add a Failed Jobs page with list, show detail (payload + exception), retry, and delete actions
verification: Test with seeded failed jobs

findings.title: Subscription list has no export capability
findings.description: The Billing Subscriptions list page has search, filter, and sort but no CSV export. Financial/subscription data export is a common admin need for reconciliation and reporting.

evidence.note: No ExportButton in PageHeader actions
evidence.proof: PageHeader actions only contain 'Back to Billing' link (line 37-39)

implementation.approach: Add export endpoint following audit log export pattern
verification: Test CSV download with filters

findings.title: No admin-initiated password reset for users
findings.description: Admin cannot trigger a password reset email for a user or force-set a new password. This is a common support workflow when users are locked out.

evidence.note: User detail page has no password reset action

implementation.approach: Add 'Send Password Reset' button on User Show page that dispatches a password reset notification
verification: Test password reset email is sent

findings.title: Audit log entries do not capture before/after values for mutations
findings.description: When an admin toggles admin status or deactivates a user, the audit log records the action and the new state, but not the previous state. This makes it impossible to determine what changed from the audit trail alone. For regulatory/compliance audits, before/after capture is essential.

evidence.note: Logs 'is_admin' => $user->is_admin (new value) but not the old value
AuditService::log() accepts flat metadata array with no before/after structure

implementation.approach: Add before/after capture pattern to admin mutations. Store as metadata keys 'old_value' and 'new_value' or a 'changes' object.
changes: Capture old values before mutation in AdminUsersController
Update AuditLog Show page to display changes
verification: Verify audit log detail shows old→new values

findings.title: No failed job visibility beyond count — no list, detail, retry, or clear
findings.description: The System page shows failed_jobs count but provides no operational tooling. This forces admins to SSH into the server for job management, which is an operational blind spot.

evidence.note: Only shows count, no list/retry/clear actions

implementation.approach: Create a dedicated Failed Jobs admin page with list, detail, retry, and delete capabilities
verification: Seed failed jobs and verify list/retry/delete work

findings.title: No audit log for admin page views or data exports
findings.description: Viewing sensitive user data (User Show page) and exporting audit logs are not themselves audit-logged. An admin viewing PII or exporting data leaves no trail.

evidence.note: show() method does not log the view action
export() does not log that a data export was performed

implementation.approach: Add audit log entries for sensitive data access (user detail view, data exports)
verification: Verify audit entries appear after viewing user and exporting

findings.title: Feature-gated admin sections are stats-only with no management capabilities — classic AI-built pattern
findings.description: AI tends to build dashboard pages that display aggregate stats but never implements the operational tooling needed to act on those stats. The Webhooks, Tokens, Social Auth, Two-Factor, and Notifications admin sections all follow this pattern: show counts/charts but provide no way to manage individual records.

evidence.note: Stats + most_active list, but no revoke/view individual token
Stats + delivery chart + recent failures, but no retry/disable

implementation.approach: Prioritize adding management actions for tokens (revoke) and webhooks (disable endpoint, retry delivery) as these are most operationally critical
changes: Add CRUD routes for tokens and webhook endpoints
verification: Feature tests for admin management actions

findings.title: No data integrity tools — orphan detection, consistency checks
findings.description: AI-built admin panels rarely include data health tooling. There are no tools to detect orphaned records (subscriptions without users, tokens for deleted users, webhook deliveries for removed endpoints), stale data, or inconsistencies.

evidence.note: No data integrity or consistency check controller exists

implementation.approach: Add a Data Health page to the admin System section that runs integrity checks
verification: Seed orphan records and verify detection

findings.title: No admin notification/alert system for critical events
findings.description: When the webhook failure rate spikes, failed jobs accumulate, or health checks fail, there is no mechanism to alert admins beyond them manually checking the dashboard. AI-built panels show data but never implement proactive alerting.

evidence.note: Health page shows current status but has no threshold-based alerting

implementation.approach: Add admin notification preferences and threshold-based alerts for critical metrics
verification: Test alert triggers

findings.title: Admin pages are not wrapped in ErrorBoundary
findings.description: An ErrorBoundary component exists at Components/ui/error-boundary.tsx but is not used. AdminLayout renders children directly. A runtime error in any admin page crashes the entire page with a white screen.

evidence.note: No ErrorBoundary wrapping children
ErrorBoundary component exists but is unused

implementation.approach: Wrap {children} in AdminLayout with ErrorBoundary
verification: Manually trigger error and verify graceful fallback

findings.title: Keyboard shortcuts only on Users Index — missing from Audit Logs and Subscriptions
findings.description: useAdminKeyboardShortcuts provides / for search focus and n/p for pagination. Only wired up on Users Index. Audit Logs and Billing Subscriptions have search and pagination but no shortcuts.

evidence.note: Only page using useAdminKeyboardShortcuts

implementation.approach: Add useAdminKeyboardShortcuts to AuditLogs/Index and Billing/Subscriptions
changes: Import and configure hook in both pages
verification: Verify / focuses search and n/p navigates pages

findings.title: Billing Subscriptions page has no Clear Filters button or empty state action
findings.description: When filters return zero results, the empty state provides no way to clear filters. Users and Audit Logs pages both provide Clear Filters. This is a UX dead end.

evidence.note: AdminDataTable has no emptyAction prop

implementation.approach: Add clearFilters from useAdminFilters and pass emptyAction to AdminDataTable
changes: Add emptyAction with Clear Filters button
verification: Apply filters that return 0 results, verify Clear Filters button appears

findings.title: Audit Logs date/userId filters use mixed local/URL state model
findings.description: The from, to, and userId filters are local React state that only syncs to URL on Apply Filters click. Typed-but-unapplied values are lost on navigation with no warning.

evidence.note: Local useState for from/to/userId, synced on Apply click

implementation.approach: Either sync to URL on change (like search debounce) or add visual indicator for unapplied filters
changes: Consider debounced URL sync for date inputs or add dirty indicator
verification: Type date, navigate away, come back — verify state

findings.title: Feature Flags page lacks navigation loading overlay
findings.description: No useNavigationState usage. Mutations show loading via ConfirmDialog spinner but page content doesn't indicate in-progress state.

evidence.note: No useNavigationState import or isNavigating usage

implementation.approach: Add useNavigationState and apply opacity fade during mutations
verification: Toggle a flag and verify visual feedback

findings.title: Focus lost after successful bulk deactivation — action bar removed from DOM
findings.description: After bulk deactivation, selectedIds is cleared which removes the bulk action bar. The focus target no longer exists, leaving focus in limbo.

evidence.note: setSelectedIds(new Set()) removes action bar from DOM

implementation.approach: After clearing selection, programmatically focus the search input or table
changes: Add searchInputRef.current?.focus() after setSelectedIds in executeBulkDeactivate
verification: Tab through bulk flow, verify focus moves to search after completion

findings.title: toggleAdmin route lacks withTrashed — 404 on soft-deleted users while UI still shows action
findings.description: The toggle-admin route doesn't have ->withTrashed() so toggling admin on a deactivated user returns 404. But the UI shows Make Admin/Remove Admin for all users including deactivated ones.

evidence.note: No ->withTrashed() on toggle-admin route
Toggle admin action shown for all users

implementation.approach: Hide toggle-admin action in UI when user is deactivated. Alternatively add withTrashed to the route.
verification: Verify deactivated user has no toggle-admin action in dropdown

findings.title: AuditLog::user() relationship missing withTrashed — deleted users show as System
findings.description: Audit log entries from soft-deleted users render as 'System' because the belongsTo relationship doesn't include withTrashed(). This makes the audit trail misleading.

evidence.note: belongsTo(User::class) without withTrashed()

implementation.approach: Add withTrashed to the AuditLog::user() relationship
verification: Soft-delete a user, verify their audit logs still show their name

findings.title: bulkDeactivate uses inline validation instead of Form Request
findings.description: All other admin methods use Form Requests but bulkDeactivate uses inline $request->validate(). Breaks the codebase convention.

evidence.note: Inline validation

implementation.approach: Create AdminBulkDeactivateRequest form request
verification: php artisan test --filter=bulk

findings.title: Cross-page user selection persists invisibly — potential accidental bulk action
findings.description: Selected user IDs persist when navigating between pages. Users selected on page 1 remain in the set on page 2 but are invisible. The count includes them.

evidence.note: selectedIds not cleared on page change

implementation.approach: Clear selectedIds when page changes, or add visual indicator of cross-page selections
changes: Add useEffect that clears selectedIds when users.current_page changes
verification: Select users on page 1, navigate to page 2, verify selection is cleared

findings.title: No frontend self-action prevention on User Show page for toggle-admin and deactivate
findings.description: On User Show, Make Admin/Remove Admin and Deactivate buttons are shown even when viewing your own profile. Backend prevents the action but UX is poor — admin clicks, confirms, then sees error.

evidence.note: Buttons always shown regardless of user.id === currentUserId

implementation.approach: Disable or hide self-action buttons on Show page
changes: Add user.id !== currentUserId check for toggle-admin and deactivate buttons
verification: View own profile, verify buttons are disabled/hidden

findings.title: Single-admin lockout risk — no minimum admin count check
findings.description: toggleAdmin prevents self-demotion but not demoting the last other admin. If only two admins exist and one demotes the other, only one remains. Loss of that account means no admin access without DB manipulation.

evidence.note: No check for minimum admin count

implementation.approach: Before removing admin status, check if there would be at least 2 admins remaining (or 1 if configured)
changes: Add admin count check: if ($user->is_admin && User::where('is_admin', true)->count() <= 2) abort
verification: Test: create 2 admins, try to remove admin from one, verify rejection

findings.title: Feature Flags fetch() calls don't check response.ok for HTTP errors
findings.description: loadUserOverrides and handleSearchUsers use fetch() which doesn't reject on 4xx/5xx. Error responses could be silently processed as data.

evidence.note: No response.ok check before json parsing

implementation.approach: Add response.ok check before processing response
changes: if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Request failed') after each fetch()
verification: Test with invalid flag name, verify error toast shown

verified_good: Admin routes protected by auth + verified + admin middleware with rate limiting
Comprehensive self-action prevention (cannot toggle own admin, deactivate self, impersonate self)
Impersonation safety: cannot impersonate admins, deactivated users, or nest impersonation
Encrypted admin ID in session during impersonation with decrypt error handling
Stop-impersonation validates admin still exists and is still admin
All SQL inputs properly parameterized via Eloquent — no injection risk
No dangerouslySetInnerHTML in admin pages — React JSX escaping handles all output
CSRF protection via Inertia router for all mutations
Flash message sanitization with e() helper for user names
Empty state handling across all tables and charts
Pagination with proper URL persistence and page reset on filter change
Debounced search (300ms) via useAdminFilters hook
Confirmation dialogs on all destructive actions with loading states
Loading overlays during navigation on list pages
Bulk deactivation excludes self, admins, and already-deactivated users in DB transaction
Feature flag route parameter constrained with regex [a-z_]+
Sort columns validated against allowlists
Audit logging on all admin mutations with IP and user agent
CSV export has formula injection protection
Admin sidebar navigation properly feature-gated
Billing admin handles soft-deleted users with withTrashed and null-safe operators
>90% structural uniformity across admin pages using shared components
Shared AdminDataTable, AdminStatsGrid, SortHeader, AdminCharts components
Consistent icon library (Lucide React) and sizing
No placeholder content across any admin page
Accessible: ARIA labels, sr-only legends, keyboard-navigable
